query.name: Graph Query
query.text2gql_description: This is a UI preview for a future Text2GQL workflow. It is not connected to a model or query service.
query.text2gql_placeholder: Describe the graph question you want to ask
query.text2gql_privacy: Nothing entered here is sent, generated, saved, or executed.
query.execute_mode_desc: Query mode is for small analyses that return within 30 seconds. Task mode is for larger analyses that take longer; task details are available in Task Management.

query_result.run_failed_action: Query failed. Review the statement and try again.
query_result.no_graph_result: No graph result. Check Table or Json data.
query_result.no_table_result: No table result. Check Graph or Json data.
query_result.no_json_result: No Json result. Check Graph or Table data.

clear_graph.title: Clear the current canvas?
clear_graph.description: Clear the current canvas. This action cannot be undone.

tooltip.import_2d: The current graph already has data. Clear it before importing.
tooltip.import_3d: Import is not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.export_3d: Export is not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.style_3d: Style settings are not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.filter_3d: Filter is not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.layout_2d: No graph data to lay out.
tooltip.layout_3d: Layout settings are not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.setting_3d: Settings are not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.new_3d: New graph elements are not supported in 3D mode.
tooltip.statistics_3d: Statistics are not supported in 3D mode.

layout_panel.title: Layout
layout_panel.enable_layout_tooltip: Enable layout. When disabled, the graph uses the default layout.
layout_panel.node_size_tooltip: Node size (diameter), used for collision detection.
layout_panel.node_strength_tooltip: Node force. Positive values attract nodes, and negative values repel nodes.
layout_panel.node_spacing_tooltip: Takes effect when preventOverlap is enabled. Minimum spacing between node edges.
layout_panel.start_radius_tooltip: Start radius of the spiral layout
layout_panel.end_radius_tooltip: End radius of the spiral layout
layout_panel.divisions_tooltip: Number of divisions on the circle
layout_panel.angle_ratio_tooltip: How many 2 * PI intervals exist from the first node to the last node
layout_panel.ordering_tooltip: Ordering basis for nodes on the circle
layout_panel.sweep_tooltip: Sweep between the first node and the last node.
layout_panel.ranksep_tooltip: Layer spacing in px. For TB or BT, it is vertical layer spacing. For LR or RL, it is horizontal layer spacing.
layout_panel.nodesep_tooltip: Node spacing in px. For TB or BT, it is horizontal node spacing. For LR or RL, it is vertical node spacing.
layout_panel.rows_tooltip: Number of grid rows. When undefined, the algorithm calculates it from node count, layout space, and cols if specified.
layout_panel.cols_tooltip: Number of grid columns. When undefined, the algorithm calculates it from node count, layout space, and rows if specified.
layout_panel.unit_radius_tooltip: Distance between each ring and the previous ring. By default, it fills the canvas based on graph size.
layout_panel.focus_node_tooltip: Radiation center. Defaults to the first node in data. A node id or node object can be passed.
layout_panel.radial_node_spacing_tooltip: Takes effect when preventOverlap is true. Minimum spacing between node edges.
layout_panel.radial_prevent_overlap_tooltip: Whether to prevent overlap. Use with nodeSize. Collision detection works only when nodeSize matches the current graph node size.
layout_panel.strict_radial_tooltip: Whether the layout must be a strict radial layout

statistics_panel.type_count_desc: Counts of all vertex and edge types in the current canvas.
statistics_panel.degree_top10_desc: The ten nodes with the highest one-hop edge counts in the current canvas.
statistics_panel.zero_degree_nodes_desc: Isolated nodes are nodes that are not connected to other nodes in the canvas.
statistics_panel.zero_degree_edges_desc: Edges whose endpoint nodes only have one-hop associations.
statistics_panel.incidence_nodes_desc: Select a node in the graph to show the number of its adjacent nodes.

failure_reason.GREMLIN_EXECUTION_FAILED: Query failed. Review the statement and try again.

common.sample_rate: Edge sample rate. This algorithm grows exponentially and requires high compute capacity, so choose a practical sample rate for an approximate result.
common.request_memory: Minimum Memory Required per Compute Node
common.JVM_memory: JVM memory size, 32 GB by default
common.query_tooltip: OLAP algorithms can be used only after the selected graph data is loaded.

form.vertices_required: Specify ids, or specify both label and properties
form.vertices_ids_tooltip: Provide source or target vertices by vertex ID list. If ids is empty, label and properties are used together to query vertices.
form.vertices_label_tooltip: Vertex label. label and properties take effect only when ids is empty.
form.vertices_properties_tooltip: Property map. Keys are property names and values are property values defined by schema. Values can be lists; a property matches when its value is in the list.

step.steps: Step list from the source vertex
step.direction: Traversal direction from the source vertex
step.edge_properties: Property map used to filter edges. Keys are property names and values are property values defined by schema. Values can be lists; a property matches when its value is in the list.
step.max_times: Repeat count for the current step. N means this step can be repeated 1 to N times from the source vertex.
step.max_degree: Maximum adjacent edges traversed from a single vertex during the query
step.max_degree_compatible: Maximum adjacent edges traversed from a single vertex. Before 0.12, step only supported degree; since 0.12 it uses max_degree and remains backward compatible with degree.
step.skip_degree: Minimum edge count for skipping super vertices. When a vertex has more adjacent edges than skip_degree, the vertex is skipped. Optional; when enabled, skip_degree must be >= max_degree. Default 0 disables skipping. Enabling it may add traversal overhead.

validation.range_or_minus_one: Enter -1 or an integer greater than 0
validation.max_depth_range: Enter an integer in the range (0, 5000]
validation.max_limit_range: Enter an integer in the range (0, 800000)
validation.non_negative_integer: Enter an integer greater than or equal to 0
validation.skip_degree_range: Enter an integer in the range [0, 10000000]
validation.alpha_range: Enter a number in the range (0, 1]
validation.top_range: Enter a number in the range (0, 1000)
validation.open_unit_range: Enter a number in the range (0, 1)
validation.depth_2_50: Enter an integer in the range [2, 50]
validation.properties_format: Enter one key=value pair per line
validation.string_value_quote: Wrap string values in single quotes
validation.open_unit_value: Enter a value in the range (0, 1)
validation.closed_unit_value: Enter a value in the range [0, 1]
validation.integer_0_100000: Enter an integer in the range [0, 100000]

capacity_item.tooltip: Maximum number of vertices visited during traversal

label_item.tooltip: Edge type. Leave empty to include all edge labels.

max_degree_item.tooltip: Maximum adjacent edges traversed from a single vertex during the query

nearest_item.tooltip: When nearest is true, the shortest path length from the source vertex to the result vertex is depth and no shorter path exists. When nearest is false, there is a path with length depth from the source vertex to the result vertex, but it may not be shortest and may contain cycles.

betweenness_centrality.desc: Betweenness Centrality estimates how likely a node is to act as a bridge on paths between other nodes. Higher values indicate stronger bridge behavior, such as common followed accounts in a social network.
betweenness_centrality.sample_rate_long: Edge sample rate. This algorithm grows exponentially and requires high compute capacity, so choose a practical sample rate for an approximate result.
betweenness_centrality.use_endpoint: Include the last vertex in computation

closeness_centrality.desc: Computes the reciprocal of the shortest distance from a node to all other reachable nodes, accumulates the values, and normalizes the result. It is used to calculate closeness centrality for each node and supports directed and undirected graphs.
closeness_centrality.wf_improved: Use the Wasserman and Faust closeness centrality formula

cluster_coefficient.desc: Clustering coefficient computes the local clustering coefficient for each vertex. Global clustering coefficient is not available yet.
cluster_coefficient.minimum_edges_use_superedge_cache: Use memory to reduce message volume. If memory is insufficient, change 100 to 1000, but the clustering coefficient job may not complete.

degree_centrality.desc: Computes degree centrality for each node and supports directed and undirected graphs.
degree_centrality.direction: Direction: in/out/both for incoming, outgoing, or both directions

filtered_rings_detection.desc: Filtered Rings Detection detects rings in the graph. Each ring path is recorded by the vertex with the smallest ID in the ring. Vertex and edge property filters can be specified to selectively propagate paths.

filter_subgraph_matching.desc: Filter Subgraph Matching accepts a query graph with property filters and matches all subgraphs that are isomorphic to the query graph.
filter_subgraph_matching.query_graph_config: Query graph configuration as a JSON array string

k_core.desc: K-Core marks all vertices whose degree is K.
k_core.k: K value for the K-Core algorithm. Optional, with a default value.

label_propagation_algorithm.desc: Label Propagation Algorithm is a graph clustering algorithm commonly used in social networks.

links.desc: Links tracing starts from a set of source vertices, propagates according to specified rules, stops when the end condition is met, and records the resulting paths.

louvain.desc: Louvain is a modularity-based community detection algorithm. Because of its algorithm characteristics, it runs with only one worker instance.

computer_item.master_request_memory: Master Minimum Memory. Allocation fails if the minimum is not met.
computer_item.worker_request_memory: Worker Minimum Memory. Allocation fails if the minimum is not met.
computer_item.master_memory: Master Max Memory. The task is terminated by Kubernetes if this limit is exceeded.
computer_item.worker_memory: Worker Max Memory. The task is terminated by Kubernetes if this limit is exceeded.

item.SINGLESOURCESHORTESTPATH: Find Shortest Paths from One Vertex
item.MULTINODESSHORTESTPATH: Find Shortest Paths for Specified Vertices

page_rank.desc: PageRank ranks web pages or graph nodes by computing relationships through links, reflecting node relevance and importance.
page_rank.alpha: Weight coefficient, also known as damping factor
page_rank.l1: Convergence precision. It is the upper bound of the accumulated absolute changes between two iterations. The algorithm stops when the value is below this threshold.
page_rank.damping: Damping factor, the percentage propagated to the next node
page_rank.diff: Convergence precision. It is the upper bound of the accumulated absolute changes between two iterations. The algorithm stops when the value is below this threshold.

personal_page_bank.desc: PersonalPageRank is a personalized recommendation algorithm that computes relevance and importance from links between pages or graph nodes.
personal_page_bank.alpha: Weight coefficient, also known as damping factor
personal_page_bank.use_id_fixlength: When true, the system uses auto-increment ID calculation
personal_page_bank.use_id_fixlength_query: Whether to use auto-increment ID calculation

ring_detection.desc: Rings Detection detects rings in the graph. Each ring path is recorded by the vertex with the smallest ID in the ring.

SSSPVermeer.desc: Single Source Shortest Path computes the shortest distance from one vertex to all other vertices.

triangle_count.desc: Triangle Count computes the number of triangles that each vertex participates in.
triangle_count.minimum_edges_use_superedge_cache: Use memory to reduce message volume. If memory is insufficient, change 100 to 1000, but the triangle count job may not complete.

weakly_connected_component.desc: Weakly Connected Component computes all connected subgraphs in an undirected graph and outputs the weakly connected component ID for each vertex.

common.direction_source_target: Direction from the source vertex to the target vertex. The reverse direction is target to source. BOTH ignores direction.
common.weight_property: Edge weight property. The property type must be numeric. If it is empty or an edge does not have the property, the weight is 1.0.
common.alpha: Probability of walking outward from a vertex in each iteration, similar to alpha in PageRank.
common.source_target_path: Path traversed from the source vertex to the target vertex

template_paths.desc: Find all paths that match a set of source vertices, edge rules including direction, labels, and property filters, and max depth.
template_paths.with_ring: true includes rings; false excludes rings
template_paths.capacity: Maximum number of vertices visited during traversal

kout_get.desc: Find vertices reachable from the source vertex in exactly depth steps, using optional direction and edge label filters.

kneighbor_get.desc: Find all vertices reachable within depth steps from the source vertex, including the source vertex, using optional direction and edge label filters.

kout_post.desc: Find vertices reachable from the source vertex in exactly depth steps using step rules.
kout_post.capacity: Maximum number of vertices visited during traversal
kout_post.algorithm: Traversal strategy. deep_first usually performs better, but when nearest is true it may include non-nearest vertices, especially on large datasets.

same_neighbors.desc: Find common neighbors of two vertices.
same_neighbors.limit: Maximum number of returned common neighbors

rings.desc: Find reachable rings from the source vertex using optional direction, edge label, and max depth filters.
rings.source_in_ring: Whether the ring must contain the source vertex

shortest_path.desc: Find one shortest path using source vertex, target vertex, direction, optional edge label, and max depth.

jaccard_similarity_get.desc: Compute the Jaccard similarity of two vertices: the intersection of their neighbors divided by the union of their neighbors.

crosspoints.desc: Find crosspoints using source vertex, target vertex, direction, optional edge label, and max depth.

find_shortest_path.desc: Find all shortest paths between two vertices using source vertex, target vertex, direction, optional edge label, and max depth.

find_shortest_path_with_weight.desc: Find one weighted shortest path using source vertex, target vertex, direction, optional edge label, and max depth.

paths.desc: Find all paths using source vertex, target vertex, direction, optional edge label, and max depth.
paths.limit: Number of target vertices to query and number of returned shortest paths

rays.desc: Find paths that radiate from the source vertex to boundary vertices using optional direction, edge label, and max depth filters.

rank_api.desc: Recommend other vertices with similar or related connections based on a source vertex's existing outgoing edges.
rank_api.label: Edge label starting from the source vertex. It must connect two different vertex categories.
rank_api.sorted: Whether to sort returned results by rank
rank_api.with_label: Choose which result categories to keep: SAME_LABEL, OTHER_LABEL, or BOTH_LABEL.
rank_api.same_label: Keep only vertices with the same category as the source vertex
rank_api.other_label: Keep only vertices with a different category from the source vertex, usually the other side of a bipartite graph
rank_api.both_label: Keep vertices from both the same and opposite categories as the source vertex

neighbor_rank_api.desc: Find the top N vertices most related to the source vertex at each layer in a general graph structure, along with their relevance scores.
neighbor_rank_api.top: Keep only the top N weighted results at each layer

jaccard_similarity_post.desc: Find the top N vertices with the highest Jaccard similarity to the specified vertex.
jaccard_similarity_post.top: Return the top vertices with the highest Jaccard similarity to a source vertex

kneighbor_post.desc: Find all vertices reachable within depth steps from the source vertex using step rules, including direction, edge labels, and property filters.

single_source_shortest_path.desc: Starting from one vertex, find shortest paths from that vertex to other vertices in the graph, optionally with edge weights.

multi_nodes_shortest_path.desc: Find shortest paths between every pair in a specified vertex set.

all_paths.desc: Find all paths using source vertices, target vertices, step rules, and max depth.

fusiform_similarity.desc: Find fusiform-similar vertices for a set of source vertices under the specified conditions.
fusiform_similarity.min_neighbors: Minimum neighbor count. If a source vertex has fewer neighbors than this threshold, it is not considered to have fusiform-similar vertices.
fusiform_similarity.alpha: Similarity threshold: the ratio of common neighbors shared by the source vertex and the fusiform-similar vertex to all neighbors of the source vertex.
fusiform_similarity.min_similars: Minimum number of fusiform-similar vertices. The source vertex and its similar vertices are returned only when the count is greater than or equal to this value.
fusiform_similarity.top: Return the top N fusiform-similar vertices with the highest similarity for each source vertex. 0 means all.
fusiform_similarity.group_property: Used with min_groups. When the source vertex and its fusiform-similar vertices have at least min_groups different values for this property, the source vertex and its similar vertices are returned. Leave empty to skip this property filter.
fusiform_similarity.min_groups: Used with group_property; meaningful only when group_property is set.
fusiform_similarity.limit: Maximum number of returned result groups. One source vertex and its fusiform-similar vertices count as one result.
fusiform_similarity.with_intermediary: Whether to return intermediate vertices shared by the source vertex and its fusiform-similar vertices.

resource_allocation.desc: Resource Allocation is commonly used in social networks to estimate how closely two vertices are related based on their common neighbors.

same_neighbors_batch.desc: Batch query common neighbors for pairs of vertices.

egonet.desc: Find K-hop neighbors of multiple source vertices, with optional filters for different vertex and edge labels. Results include the source vertices.
egonet.sources: Source vertex ID list. Multiple vertices are supported.

customized_paths.desc: Find all paths that match a set of source vertices, edge rules including direction, labels, and property filters, and max depth.
customized_paths.sort_by: Sort by path weight: NONE means no sorting, INCR sorts by ascending path weight, and DECR sorts by descending path weight.
customized_paths.weight_by: Use the specified property to calculate edge weight. It is effective when sort_by is not NONE and is mutually exclusive with default_weight.
customized_paths.default_weight: Default edge weight used when an edge does not have the weight property. It is effective when sort_by is not NONE and is mutually exclusive with weight_by.
customized_paths.sample: Sample matching edges for the current step. -1 means no sampling.

customized_crosspoints.desc: Find the intersection of path endpoints that match multiple path rules, including direction, edge labels, property filters, and max depth.
customized_crosspoints.path_patterns: Path rule list traversed from the source vertex

adamic_adar.desc: Adamic-Adar is commonly used in social networks to estimate how closely two vertices are related based on the density of their common neighbors.
adamic_adar.direction: Direction from the source vertex to the target vertex. The reverse direction is target to source. BOTH ignores direction.
adamic_adar.label: Leave empty to include all edge labels
adamic_adar.max_degree: Maximum adjacent edges traversed from a single vertex during the query

async_task.name: Task Management
async_task.search_placeholder: Enter a task ID or name
async_task.delete_confirm_content: Delete this task? This cannot be undone.
async_task.batch_delete_content: Delete the selected tasks? This cannot be undone.

topbar.metadata_tooltip: Open metadata configuration for the selected graph
